causal_validation.diff_summary: Commits inspected: be472595525c361656ca88f3ed2d2861105083f0, 624cb56a4e3e9dc0f7fde6cc047085a330a28d21, 0c6384c1a22fec745d3e058bd2d37471a10c5b01, 1a605ed40b6870e6a16be0ed990b071b268985a2. src/command_metrics.rs changed from a single Sharded mode to Sharded2Key + ShardedN with key_hint routing, introduced String-keyed ShardedN (metric_key.to_string() in record path), and moved DashMap initialization from DashMap::new() to DashMap::with_shard_amount(64). benchmarks/src/main.rs changed workload measurement semantics by adding a 10% warmup phase and measuring requests_per_client directly (instead of earlier per-concurrency request splitting). Provenance check: metadata.json records commit be472595..., but that commit's source does not contain ShardedN/HdrHistogram or warmup logic, so the dataset was produced from code not represented by that clean commit hash. Repository validation: release builds for server and benchmark succeeded during fresh system-validation execution.
causal_validation.mechanism: Performance shifts are attributable to code-level changes in workload parameterization and metrics collection internals. The benchmark workload changes (warmup exclusion plus fixed per-client request semantics) directly alter measured throughput/latency behavior, while metrics keying/sharding changes modify contention characteristics (especially ShardedN key-space distribution).
